paper_title: Open problems of the 33rd Workshop on Cycles and Colourings
summary: Problem 4.1 asks whether every diamond-free claw-free cubic graph is strongly 6-edge-colorable, equivalently whether $\chi'_s(T(G))=6$ for every cubic graph $G$. Before the November 2025 posting, Han and Cui (2023) confirmed the conjecture for truncated prisms as a partial result, while Lv, Li, and Zhang (2022) proved $\chi'_s \le 8$ and Lin and Lin (2023) established the tight bound of 7 for the broader class of claw-free subcubic graphs. No post-November 2025 paper resolving the full conjecture was found.

strong edge coloring truncated prism claw-free subcubic graph strong chromatic index
Han Cui strong 6-edge-colorable truncated prism cubic graph arXiv 2024 2025
notes: The conjecture was posed in November 2025. Relevant prior work: Han and Cui (2023) proved χ'_s(T(G))=6 for all truncated n-prisms (partial case); Lin and Lin (2023) proved the tight bound 7 for claw-free subcubic graphs generally; Lv, Li, and Zhang (2022) proved the bound 8 for the same class. These papers predate the conjecture's posting and are not listed in since_posted. No follow-up paper resolving Problem 4.1 in full was found in the indexed literature.
